mnemonics: Kill sends signals to others, Raise sends signals to itself. Remember: You RAISE your own hand, you KILL others.
keyPoints: kill(pid, sig) sends a signal to a process identified by its PID.
raise(sig) sends a signal to the calling process itself.
The sig argument specifies the signal number (e.g., SIGKILL, SIGTERM, SIGUSR1).
commonMistakes: Confusing kill() with only terminating a process; it can send any signal, not just SIGKILL.
Using an incorrect PID (like 0 or -1) without understanding its special meaning for process groups.
Assuming raise(sig) is equivalent to kill(getpid(), sig) without considering possible thread-specific signal handling nuances.
